---
title: "Meeting_03_10_2025"
author: "Matthew"
output: github_document
---

# Purpose

This document summarises the empirical methods employed in Heckman (2006)

# Structural modelling of Heckman (2006)

[Here is the web appendix to the original Heckman paper](https://jenni.uchicago.edu/noncog/web_supplement.pdf)

Notes taken from __Section VI (Implementing the Model)__ of Heckman paper:

- Use Bayesian Markov chain Monte Carlo methods to compute sample likelihood
- "analysis in Carneiro (2003), Hansen (2004) and Heckman and Navarro (2006) establishes conditions on the support of the regressors that allow for semiparametric identification of the model"
- "for the support conditions for semiparametric identification to hold, the support of the distribution for each probability should be the unit interval [0,1]"

```{r echo=FALSE, out.width='50%'}
knitr::include_graphics('/Users/matthewthompson/Documents/Stellenbosch University/Masters/Research Assignment/Data_work/output/Image 2025-10-02 at 18.51.jpeg')
```

- Wage equations at age 30 estimated for individuals at each final schooling level
- Dummies included:
    - Race
    - Cohort (Age/Year of birth)
    - Local labour market conditions (check Cameron and Heckman (2001) for list of variables used)
    - Region of residence
- Multivariate probit model used
